expectations.text: Recommends keeping competition lifts in moderate strength-relevant rep ranges rather than going entirely high-rep
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 includes '2–3 singles at 80–85% 1RM' and '3–4 sets of 3–5 reps at 70–75% 1RM.' Phase 3 maintains '2 singles at 80% 1RM (technical anchor).' Competition lifts stay in strength-relevant ranges throughout.

expectations.text: Suggests a specific structure (e.g., mostly 5-10 rep range with periodic heavier work like triples)
expectations.evidence: Clear progression: Phase 2 uses 3-5 rep and 6-8 rep work. Phase 3 shifts to 5-6 and 6-8 rep ranges. Periodic heavy singles maintained throughout. Detailed 12-week progression table provided.

expectations.text: Mentions an over-warm or heavy single strategy (e.g., RPE 8 single) before volume work to maintain neural readiness
expectations.evidence: Phase 3: '2 singles at 80% 1RM (technical anchor)' placed before volume work. 'Technical anchor singles maintain CNS efficiency and prevent strength loss.' This matches the over-warm single concept.

expectations.text: Core message: maintain some heavy practice while accumulating hypertrophy volume
expectations.evidence: 'This approach preserves your meet-prep strength gains while building hypertrophy sustainably.' Strength Preservation Tactics section explicitly addresses keeping heavy work alongside volume.
